16.reason: [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings depending on compiler settings. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it can cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, 'gets' is removed entirely, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings, and many modern compilers (like GCC) no longer support it by default.]

36.reason: [When the '-' is not expanded, this prints three characters (previous, '-', next), but in subsequent iterations, the next character will be printed again, causing duplication. Also, if '-' is at the start or end, accessing str[i-1] or str[i+1] leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When the '-' is not expanded, this prints three characters (previous, '-', next), but in subsequent iterations, the next character will be printed again, causing duplication. Also, if '-' is at the start or end, accessing str[i-1] or str[i+1] leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering an unexpandable '-', this prints three characters (previous, '-', next), but in subsequent iterations, the next character will be printed again, causing duplication. Also, if '-' is at the start or end, accessing str[i-1] or str[i+1] leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints three characters when '-' should not be expanded, but it duplicates adjacent characters on repeated non-expandable '-' cases. Also, when i=0 or i=len-1, str[i-1] or str[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior. However, the immediate cause of the compilation error is 'gets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When '-' is not expanded, this prints three characters (including neighbors), but those neighbors will also be printed again when the loop reaches their indices, causing duplication.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When '-' is not expanded, this prints three characters (previous, '-', next), but in subsequent iterations, the next character will be printed again, causing duplication. Also, if '-' appears at the start or end, str[i-1] or str[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.]
